automation.title: Testing Fundamentals and QA Mindset
automation.description: Understand the core principles of software testing, including test types, defect lifecycle, and QA goals. Build a solid Python foundation and set up a local testing environment.
automation.challenge: Write a Python test suite using Pytest that verifies a simple calculator module with at least 10 test cases covering edge cases.

automation.title: Selenium WebDriver Basics
automation.description: Learn to drive browsers programmatically using Selenium WebDriver with Python bindings. Practice locating elements, interacting with forms, and handling dynamic content.

resources.label: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python

automation.challenge: Automate a login flow on a demo site, verify successful login, then log out - handling all waits and assertions properly.

automation.title: TestNG Framework and Test Organization
automation.description: Adopt TestNG as your test framework to organize, group, and prioritize tests effectively. Learn Java fundamentals needed to write structured TestNG test classes.
automation.challenge: Create a TestNG project with at least 3 test groups, a shared data provider, and a suite XML that runs groups in a specific order.

automation.title: Page Object Model and Test Design Patterns
automation.description: Apply the Page Object Model pattern to make your Selenium tests maintainable and readable. Learn to separate test logic from page interaction code.
topics: Separation of concerns in test code

resources.label: POM Tutorial with Python and Selenium

automation.challenge: Refactor a spaghetti Selenium script into a full POM structure with at least 3 page classes and a shared BasePage.

automation.title: Playwright for Modern Web Automation
automation.description: Adopt Playwright as a modern, fast alternative to Selenium with built-in waits and multi-browser support. Learn its API for network interception, screenshots, and tracing.
automation.challenge: Write a Playwright test suite that covers a multi-step checkout flow, intercepts a payment API call, and records a trace on failure.

automation.title: API Testing and Integration
automation.description: Test REST APIs directly using the Python Requests library and integrate API assertions into your test suites. Understand request construction, response validation, and authentication patterns.
topics: Combining API and UI test flows
automation.challenge: Build a Pytest suite that validates all CRUD operations on a public API, including schema checks and auth token handling.

automation.title: Data-Driven Testing and Test Management
automation.description: Drive test execution from external data sources like CSV, Excel, and databases to maximize coverage without code duplication. Integrate with test management tools to track results.
topics: Reading test data from CSV and Excel
automation.challenge: Create a data-driven Playwright test that reads 20 login scenarios from a CSV file and generates a formatted Allure report.

automation.title: Cross-Browser and Visual Testing
automation.description: Scale your tests across multiple browsers and devices using cloud grids and visual regression tools. Detect layout regressions automatically with screenshot comparison.
automation.challenge: Configure a Playwright project to run the same suite against Chrome, Firefox, and Safari in parallel on BrowserStack.

automation.title: Advanced Patterns: Fixtures, Hooks, and Reporting
automation.description: Master advanced Pytest features including fixtures with scope, conftest organization, and custom hooks for pre/post test logic. Build rich, stakeholder-friendly HTML reports.
topics: Pytest fixture scopes and dependency injection
automation.challenge: Refactor an existing test suite to use scoped fixtures for DB setup, enable parallel execution, and produce an Allure report with attachments on failure.

automation.title: CI/CD Integration and Production Pipeline
automation.description: Embed your automation suite into a CI/CD pipeline so tests run on every pull request and deployment. Containerize your test environment with Docker for consistent, reproducible runs.
automation.challenge: Build a GitHub Actions pipeline that runs your Playwright suite in Docker across 3 shards, posts results to Slack, and blocks merge on failure.

manual.title: QA Foundations and Testing Principles
manual.description: Establish a strong understanding of software quality assurance principles, methodologies, and the tester mindset. Learn the software development lifecycle and where QA fits in.
manual.challenge: Write a one-page test plan for a hypothetical login feature including scope, approach, entry/exit criteria, and risk identification.

manual.title: Test Case Design Techniques
manual.description: Learn systematic techniques for designing test cases that maximize coverage while minimizing redundancy. Apply equivalence partitioning, boundary analysis, and decision tables to real features.
manual.challenge: Design a complete test case set for a registration form using at least 3 design techniques and document each case in TestRail.

manual.title: Jira for Bug Tracking and Project Management
manual.description: Master Jira as a bug tracker and project management tool for Agile QA workflows. Learn to write high-quality bug reports that developers can act on immediately.
topics: Sprint ceremonies from a QA perspective

resources.label: How to Write a Good Bug Report

manual.challenge: File 5 detailed bug reports for a live demo application in Jira, each with steps to reproduce, expected vs actual results, and severity justification.

manual.title: TestRail for Test Management
manual.description: Use TestRail to organize test suites, manage test runs, and track test coverage over time. Learn to create meaningful test execution reports for stakeholders.
manual.challenge: Set up a TestRail project for a sample application, import test cases, execute a test run, and produce a coverage report.

manual.title: API Testing with Postman
manual.description: Learn to test REST APIs manually using Postman without writing code. Understand HTTP fundamentals, request construction, and how to validate API responses.
topics: HTTP methods, headers, and status codes
manual.challenge: Build a Postman collection that covers all CRUD operations on a public API with environment variables, pre-request scripts, and test assertions.

manual.title: Database Testing with SQL
manual.description: Validate application data integrity by querying databases directly using SQL. Learn to verify that front-end actions produce correct database changes.
topics: Aggregation functions: COUNT, SUM, GROUP BY
Comparing UI data with DB records
Using DBeaver as a database client
manual.challenge: Given a demo e-commerce app, write SQL queries that verify order totals, user account balances, and product inventory after a series of manual transactions.

manual.title: Regression and Smoke Testing Strategies
manual.description: Design efficient regression and smoke test suites that protect release quality without slowing delivery. Learn prioritization techniques based on risk and change impact.
topics: Smoke vs sanity vs regression testing
manual.challenge: Given a 200-case test suite, identify the 30 highest-risk cases for a smoke suite using a documented risk scoring method.

manual.title: Exploratory Testing and Session-Based Testing
manual.description: Go beyond scripted tests with structured exploratory testing techniques that uncover defects traditional test cases miss. Use session-based test management to bring discipline to exploration.
topics: Note-taking and bug capture during sessions
manual.challenge: Run three 45-minute exploratory testing sessions on a live app using charters, capture notes, file bugs, and write a debrief summary.

manual.title: Usability, Accessibility, and Cross-Browser Testing
manual.description: Expand your testing scope beyond functionality to cover usability, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser consistency. Learn to evaluate UI experiences and identify WCAG violations manually.
manual.challenge: Audit a public website for WCAG 2.1 AA violations manually and with WAVE, then document and prioritize findings in Jira.

manual.title: Release QA, Metrics, and Process Ownership
manual.description: Own the end-to-end quality process for a release cycle, from planning through sign-off. Build QA dashboards and metrics that communicate release health to stakeholders.
topics: Defect density and escape rate metrics
manual.challenge: Lead a simulated release cycle: create a test plan, execute tests, track defects, build a release metrics dashboard, and write a go/no-go recommendation.

performance.title: Performance Testing Fundamentals
performance.description: Understand the goals, types, and key metrics of performance testing before writing a single test. Learn to distinguish load, stress, spike, soak, and scalability tests.
topics: Performance test types and when to use them
Key metrics: throughput, latency, error rate
Response time percentiles: p50, p90, p99
HTTP protocol basics for performance testers

resources.label: The Art of Application Performance Testing

performance.challenge: Install JMeter, record a simple HTTP test plan against a demo site, and document the key metrics observed during a 10-user load test.

performance.title: JMeter Test Plan Design
performance.description: Build structured JMeter test plans with thread groups, samplers, listeners, and timers. Learn to parameterize tests with CSV data and use assertions for correctness.
topics: Config elements: HTTP Header Manager, Cookie Manager
CSV Data Set Config for parameterization
View Results Tree and Summary Report listeners
performance.challenge: Build a JMeter test plan that simulates 50 virtual users logging in with unique credentials from a CSV and validates each response.

performance.title: k6 for Modern Load Testing
performance.description: Adopt k6 as a developer-friendly load testing tool where tests are written as JavaScript scripts. Integrate k6 output with InfluxDB and Grafana for real-time dashboards.
performance.challenge: Write a k6 script that ramps from 0 to 100 users, defines SLA thresholds for p95 latency and error rate, and streams results to a Grafana dashboard.

performance.title: Gatling for Scala-Based Load Testing
performance.description: Use Gatling to write expressive load test scenarios in Scala with a DSL designed for readability. Generate rich HTML reports automatically after each test run.
performance.challenge: Build a Gatling simulation for a REST API that uses a feeder, runs a ramp-up scenario of 200 users, and includes assertions on p99 response time.

performance.title: Grafana Dashboards and Observability
performance.description: Build Grafana dashboards that provide real-time visibility into load test runs and application performance. Learn to correlate load patterns with application metrics.
topics: Correlating load test data with app metrics
performance.challenge: Create a Grafana dashboard that displays k6 throughput, error rate, p95 latency, and application CPU/memory metrics side by side during a live test.

performance.title: Profiling and Bottleneck Analysis
performance.description: Go beyond collecting metrics to diagnosing the root cause of performance problems. Learn to profile JVM applications, analyze slow SQL queries, and identify resource saturation.
performance.challenge: Run a load test against a deliberately slow demo app, identify the bottleneck using profiling tools, and write a root cause analysis report.

performance.title: Stress, Spike, and Soak Testing
performance.description: Design specialized test scenarios beyond simple load tests to validate system resilience under extreme conditions. Learn what to measure and when to stop each test type.
topics: Stress testing to find the breaking point
Defining pass/fail criteria per test type
Capacity planning from stress test data

resources.label: Stress vs Load vs Soak Testing

performance.challenge: Design and execute a soak test running for 2 hours, monitor for memory leaks and error rate degradation, and document findings with Grafana screenshots.

performance.title: API and Microservices Performance Testing
performance.description: Test performance at the API and microservice layer independently of the UI to get faster, more targeted results. Learn distributed tracing concepts to follow requests across services.
performance.challenge: Write k6 tests that independently load-test 3 microservices, compare their p99 latencies, and correlate with distributed traces for the slowest endpoint.

performance.title: Performance Test Reporting and SLA Definition
performance.description: Translate raw performance numbers into actionable insights for development teams and management. Learn to define SLAs, write performance test reports, and track trends over releases.
topics: Defining SLAs and SLOs for APIs

resources.label: SLA vs SLO vs SLI Guide

performance.challenge: Write a complete performance test report comparing two releases, including trend charts, SLA compliance table, and a recommendation section.

performance.title: CI/CD Integration and Continuous Performance Testing
performance.description: Embed performance tests into your CI/CD pipeline so every deployment is automatically validated against performance thresholds. Block releases that violate SLA automatically.
performance.challenge: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs k6 smoke and load tests on every PR, fails the build if p95 exceeds threshold, and posts a Grafana snapshot as a comment.

security-qa.title: Web Security Fundamentals for Testers
security-qa.description: Build a mental model of web application security by studying the OWASP Top 10 and common vulnerability classes. Learn how HTTP, cookies, and sessions work from an attacker perspective.
topics: Attacker mindset and threat modeling intro
security-qa.challenge: Map all OWASP Top 10 items to a sample application and document which attack surfaces exist for each category.

security-qa.title: Burp Suite for Manual Security Testing
security-qa.description: Use Burp Suite Community Edition as an intercepting proxy to manually inspect, modify, and replay HTTP requests. Understand the Burp Suite workflow for manual vulnerability hunting.
security-qa.challenge: Use Burp Suite Repeater to manually test for SQL injection on a deliberately vulnerable app like DVWA and document your findings.

security-qa.title: OWASP ZAP for DAST Scanning
security-qa.description: Automate security scanning using OWASP ZAP as a Dynamic Application Security Testing tool. Learn to run passive and active scans and interpret ZAP reports.
topics: ZAP alerts: risk and confidence levels
security-qa.challenge: Run a full ZAP active scan against a deliberately vulnerable app, triage all high and medium alerts, and write a vulnerability report.

security-qa.title: Injection Vulnerabilities: SQL, XSS, Command
security-qa.description: Deep-dive into the most impactful injection vulnerability classes including SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting, and OS command injection. Practice in safe lab environments.
topics: SQL injection types: classic, blind, time-based
security-qa.challenge: Complete all PortSwigger Web Security Academy SQL injection and XSS labs at the apprentice level and document each exploit technique used.

security-qa.title: Authentication and Authorization Testing
security-qa.description: Test authentication mechanisms for weaknesses including brute force susceptibility, session fixation, and insecure token handling. Validate authorization controls to prevent privilege escalation.
topics: Brute force and account lockout testing
security-qa.challenge: Test a demo app for IDOR vulnerabilities and broken authentication, document at least 3 findings with CVSS scores and remediation advice.

security-qa.title: Security Test Automation with Selenium
security-qa.description: Combine functional UI automation with security scanning by running ZAP as a proxy behind Selenium tests. Detect security issues that only appear during authenticated user flows.
topics: Integrating ZAP alerts into test results
security-qa.challenge: Configure Selenium to route through ZAP during a full login and checkout flow, then extract and report all medium+ alerts found during the run.

security-qa.title: API Security Testing
security-qa.description: Apply OWASP API Security Top 10 guidance to systematically test REST APIs for vulnerabilities specific to API endpoints. Focus on broken object-level auth and mass assignment.
security-qa.challenge: Test a vulnerable API lab for all OWASP API Top 10 categories, document findings with request/response evidence and CVSS v3 scores.

security-qa.title: Security Regression and Compliance Testing
security-qa.description: Ensure that previously identified and fixed vulnerabilities do not reappear by building a security regression suite. Map tests to compliance frameworks like PCI-DSS or SOC 2.
security-qa.challenge: Build a ZAP regression scan script that checks 10 previously fixed vulnerabilities and generates a compliance-mapped report.

security-qa.title: Threat Modeling and Security Test Planning
security-qa.description: Proactively identify security risks during design by applying threat modeling frameworks. Translate threat model outputs into actionable security test cases.
topics: Data flow diagrams for threat modeling
Security test case derivation from threats
Threat model review in agile sprints
security-qa.challenge: Create a STRIDE threat model for a login and payment feature using Threat Dragon and derive at least 15 security test cases from the model.

security-qa.title: CI/CD DAST Integration and Secure SDLC
security-qa.description: Embed DAST scanning into CI/CD pipelines so security tests run automatically on every deployment. Champion a shift-left security culture by integrating security gates early in the SDLC.
topics: ZAP Docker image in CI/CD pipelines
SAST vs DAST in a pipeline
security-qa.challenge: Build a GitHub Actions pipeline that runs ZAP DAST and Snyk dependency scanning, fails on high-severity findings, and posts a security summary as a PR comment.

mobile-qa.title: Mobile Testing Fundamentals
mobile-qa.description: Understand the unique challenges of mobile testing across platforms, device types, and OS versions. Set up iOS and Android development environments for manual and automated testing.
topics: iOS Simulator and Android Emulator setup
Mobile-specific test types: gesture, orientation, interrupt
mobile-qa.challenge: Perform a manual test session on a demo Android app covering gestures, orientation changes, and interrupt scenarios, documenting all defects found.

mobile-qa.title: Appium Setup and Basic Automation
mobile-qa.description: Install and configure Appium to automate mobile apps on Android using Python. Understand Desired Capabilities and how Appium communicates with devices.
mobile-qa.challenge: Write an Appium Python script that launches a demo Android app, fills a login form, taps submit, and asserts the home screen appears.

mobile-qa.title: XCTest for iOS Native Testing
mobile-qa.description: Use Apple XCTest framework to write native UI tests for iOS applications in Swift. Learn how XCTest integrates with Xcode and the test runner.
mobile-qa.challenge: Write an XCTest UI test suite for a sample iOS app that covers 5 critical user flows and runs green in the Xcode test runner.

mobile-qa.title: Espresso for Android Native Testing
mobile-qa.description: Write fast, reliable Android UI tests using Google Espresso, which runs in-process for maximum speed. Learn Espresso matchers, actions, and assertions.
mobile-qa.challenge: Write an Espresso test suite for a sample Android app that tests a multi-screen flow including an async network call using IdlingResource.

mobile-qa.title: BrowserStack and Real Device Testing
mobile-qa.description: Run your mobile automation tests on thousands of real devices in the cloud using BrowserStack App Automate. Understand the economics and tradeoffs of real device testing.
topics: Parallel test execution on real devices
mobile-qa.challenge: Configure your Appium suite to run against 5 real Android devices on BrowserStack in parallel and compare results across device/OS combinations.

mobile-qa.title: Mobile Test Design: Gestures and Interrupts
mobile-qa.description: Test mobile-specific interactions including complex gestures, deep links, push notifications, and interrupt handling. Validate that apps behave correctly under real-world conditions.
topics: Implementing swipe, scroll, pinch with Appium
Incoming call and SMS interrupt testing
Background and foreground app state testing
mobile-qa.challenge: Write Appium tests that validate a card-swipe onboarding flow, a deep link launch, and graceful handling of an interrupt phone call.

mobile-qa.title: Mobile Performance and Battery Testing
mobile-qa.description: Evaluate mobile app performance including startup time, frame rate, memory usage, and battery drain. Use platform-native profiling tools to identify performance regressions.
mobile-qa.challenge: Profile a sample Android app during a user journey, identify one memory issue and one rendering bottleneck, and document them with profiler screenshots.

mobile-qa.title: Mobile Accessibility Testing
mobile-qa.description: Test mobile apps for accessibility compliance using screen readers and automated scanners. Ensure all interactive elements are reachable and describable.
topics: TalkBack and VoiceOver screen reader testing
mobile-qa.challenge: Perform a TalkBack accessibility audit of a sample Android app, document all accessibility barriers found, and prioritize by WCAG criterion.

mobile-qa.title: Page Object Model for Mobile Automation
mobile-qa.description: Apply Page Object Model and screen object patterns to your Appium tests to create a maintainable and scalable mobile test framework. Add reporting and parallel execution.
mobile-qa.challenge: Build a mobile test framework with screen objects, Pytest fixtures, parallel execution on 2 emulators, and Allure reporting with screenshots on failure.

mobile-qa.title: CI/CD for Mobile and Release Automation
mobile-qa.description: Automate mobile build, sign, and test workflows in CI/CD pipelines so every commit triggers tests on real devices. Use Fastlane to manage app signing and deployment.
topics: Fastlane for iOS and Android automation
App distribution to testers with Fastlane
mobile-qa.challenge: Build a GitHub Actions pipeline that builds an Android APK, uploads it to BrowserStack, runs the full Appium suite on 3 real devices, and posts results.

api-qa.title: HTTP and REST API Fundamentals
api-qa.description: Build a solid understanding of how REST APIs work including HTTP methods, status codes, headers, and request/response bodies. Learn to read API documentation and send your first requests with Postman.
topics: HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH
HTTP status codes and their meaning
api-qa.challenge: Use Postman to interact with a public REST API, performing GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE operations and documenting each response.

api-qa.title: Postman Collections and Test Scripts
api-qa.description: Organize API requests into Postman collections and write JavaScript test scripts that automatically validate responses. Use environment variables to manage multiple environments.
api-qa.challenge: Build a Postman collection with pre-request auth setup, chained requests that pass tokens between calls, and assertions on all response fields.

api-qa.title: REST Assured for Java API Testing
api-qa.description: Write expressive, readable API tests in Java using REST Assured with its fluent Given-When-Then DSL. Integrate with TestNG for test organization and reporting.
api-qa.challenge: Build a REST Assured test suite covering all CRUD operations on a sample API with JSON Schema validation on every response.

api-qa.title: API Test Design and Coverage Strategies
api-qa.description: Apply systematic test design techniques to APIs to achieve comprehensive coverage without redundancy. Learn to derive test cases from API contracts and OpenAPI specs.
topics: Positive and negative API test cases
Boundary value testing for API parameters
Error handling and error response validation
Test case traceability to API spec
Edge cases: empty body, null values, large payloads
api-qa.challenge: Derive a complete test case set for a Swagger-documented API endpoint covering all HTTP methods, parameter combinations, and documented error responses.

api-qa.title: Newman for CLI and Automated Postman Runs
api-qa.description: Run Postman collections from the command line using Newman so they can be executed in automated pipelines. Generate JUnit and HTML reports from Newman runs.
api-qa.challenge: Run a Postman collection against 3 environments using Newman, generate HTML and JUnit reports, and script a shell command that fails on test failure.

api-qa.title: API Authentication Testing
api-qa.description: Test all common authentication mechanisms used by modern APIs including API keys, JWT, OAuth2 client credentials, and session cookies. Validate token expiration and refresh flows.
topics: OAuth2 authorization code and client credentials flows
MFA and two-step auth API testing
api-qa.challenge: Write a REST Assured test that obtains a JWT via OAuth2 client credentials, uses it for protected API calls, and validates behavior after token expiry.

api-qa.title: Contract Testing with Pact
api-qa.description: Implement consumer-driven contract testing with Pact to catch API breaking changes before they reach production. Learn the consumer-provider workflow and Pact Broker for contract sharing.
api-qa.challenge: Implement a consumer Pact test for a mock service, write the provider verification, publish to Pact Broker, and run a can-I-deploy check.

api-qa.title: GraphQL API Testing
api-qa.description: Extend your API testing skills to GraphQL, understanding queries, mutations, subscriptions, and schema introspection. Identify GraphQL-specific vulnerabilities and test patterns.
api-qa.challenge: Build a Postman collection that tests queries, mutations, error cases, and introspection on a public GraphQL API with assertions on all fields.

api-qa.title: Mocking and Service Virtualization
api-qa.description: Create API mocks and stubs to enable testing when downstream services are unavailable or unpredictable. Learn service virtualization for isolated, repeatable API testing.
topics: Recording and playback of API interactions
api-qa.challenge: Set up WireMock stubs for 5 API endpoints, inject fault scenarios (delays, 500 errors), and run your REST Assured suite against the mock server.

api-qa.title: CI/CD Integration and API Test Pipelines
api-qa.description: Automate your API test suites in CI/CD pipelines that run on every pull request and deployment. Use Docker to package test environments for consistent execution.
topics: GitHub Actions for API test automation
REST Assured with Maven in CI
API test result reporting in CI
api-qa.challenge: Build a GitHub Actions pipeline that runs Newman tests against a staging environment and REST Assured contract tests, blocks merge on any failure.

sdet.title: Core Java for Test Engineering
sdet.description: Build a strong Java programming foundation specifically oriented toward test engineering. Learn OOP principles, collections, exception handling, and project structure with Maven.
sdet.challenge: Implement a Java test utility library with helper classes for data generation, HTTP client wrappers, and assertion utilities using TDD.

sdet.title: Selenium WebDriver with Java
sdet.description: Automate web browsers using Selenium WebDriver with Java bindings. Apply professional patterns like Page Object Model from day one.
topics: Screenshot capture and logging on failure
sdet.challenge: Build a Java Selenium POM framework that tests a multi-page web app, with reusable components, explicit waits, and screenshot on failure.

sdet.title: TestNG Advanced Features and Framework Design
sdet.description: Master advanced TestNG features for building production-quality test frameworks. Design a framework foundation that other engineers can contribute to.
topics: Retry analyzer for flaky test handling
Extent Reports for rich HTML reports
sdet.challenge: Build a TestNG framework with a custom listener, retry analyzer, Extent Reports integration, and a parameterized test using an external data provider.

sdet.title: API Testing with REST Assured and Java
sdet.description: Write comprehensive API tests in Java using REST Assured. Use Jackson for POJO serialization and build an API test layer that integrates with your UI framework.
topics: POJO-based request and response handling with Jackson
API test base classes and utilities
Chaining API calls in test setup
sdet.challenge: Build a Java API test layer using REST Assured and Jackson POJOs that tests all endpoints of a REST API and shares a base config with your UI tests.

sdet.title: Design Patterns in Test Automation
sdet.description: Apply software design patterns to test automation code to improve reusability, maintainability, and scalability. Learn the patterns that appear most frequently in enterprise test frameworks.
sdet.challenge: Refactor an existing Selenium framework to use Factory for browser creation, Builder for test data, and DI for shared configuration objects.

sdet.title: Unit and Integration Testing for Production Code
sdet.description: Write unit and integration tests for production Java code using JUnit 5 and Mockito. Understand the testing pyramid and how SDETs contribute to developer test coverage.
topics: Testing Spring Boot controllers with MockMvc

resources.label: Practical Unit Testing with JUnit and Mockito

sdet.challenge: Write unit tests for a provided Java service class using Mockito mocks, integration tests using Testcontainers, and measure coverage with JaCoCo.

sdet.title: BDD with Cucumber and Gherkin
sdet.description: Implement Behavior-Driven Development using Cucumber to bridge communication between business and technical teams. Write Gherkin scenarios that drive Selenium test execution.
sdet.challenge: Build a Cucumber-Selenium framework with at least 3 feature files covering a web app flow, using data tables, tags, and an HTML report plugin.

sdet.title: Test Data Management and Environments
sdet.description: Design a robust test data strategy that supports isolated, repeatable test execution across multiple environments. Use Docker to create ephemeral test environments on demand.
topics: Test data strategies: static, dynamic, on-demand
Java Faker for synthetic data generation
sdet.challenge: Build a test data factory using Java Faker that generates unique test users for each test run and cleans up after the suite completes.

sdet.title: Performance and Security Testing Integration
sdet.description: Expand SDET scope to include performance and security test contributions. Integrate Gatling performance tests and ZAP DAST scans into the Java Maven build.
topics: Gatling Java DSL for performance tests
ZAP Maven plugin for DAST scanning
Security test gates in build pipeline
sdet.challenge: Add Gatling performance tests and ZAP DAST scanning to a Maven project, configure build failure thresholds for each, and produce a combined report.

sdet.title: CI/CD Pipeline Ownership and Production Quality
sdet.description: Own the CI/CD quality gates for your team, ensuring test automation runs reliably on every build and release. Use code quality tools to keep test code as maintainable as production code.
topics: GitHub Actions for Java Maven projects
Parallel and matrix test execution in CI
sdet.challenge: Build a Jenkins pipeline that compiles code, runs unit tests, executes Selenium tests in parallel Docker containers, scans with SonarQube, and enforces a quality gate.

chaos-engineer.title: Reliability Engineering Foundations
chaos-engineer.description: Understand the principles of site reliability engineering and why chaos engineering exists. Learn to define and measure reliability through SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets.
chaos-engineer.challenge: Define SLIs and SLOs for a sample microservice application, calculate its error budget, and set up a Prometheus dashboard to track them.

chaos-engineer.title: Chaos Engineering Principles and Hypothesis
chaos-engineer.description: Study the scientific method applied to chaos engineering and learn to design rigorous experiments with clear hypotheses. Understand the steady state, blast radius, and minimal experiment concepts.
chaos-engineer.challenge: Design a chaos experiment document for a service with hypothesis, steady state, method, rollback plan, and expected learning outcome.

chaos-engineer.title: Chaos Monkey and Simian Army
chaos-engineer.description: Implement Netflix-style instance termination chaos using Chaos Monkey. Understand the Simian Army family of tools and their roles in resilience engineering.
topics: Chaos Monkey scheduling and opt-in groups
chaos-engineer.challenge: Deploy Chaos Monkey in a staging AWS environment, configure it for a test service, trigger an instance termination, and verify auto-recovery.

chaos-engineer.title: Gremlin for Targeted Chaos Experiments
chaos-engineer.description: Use Gremlin as a enterprise-grade chaos platform to run targeted resource, network, and state attacks with safety controls. Learn Gremlin attack types and scenario composition.
topics: Gremlin installation on Kubernetes and VMs
Resource attacks: CPU, memory, disk, IO
Network attacks: latency, packet loss, blackhole
chaos-engineer.challenge: Use Gremlin to run a network latency attack on a staging microservice, measure impact on dependent services via Grafana, and document recovery behavior.

chaos-engineer.title: Kubernetes Chaos with LitmusChaos
chaos-engineer.description: Run cloud-native chaos experiments directly in Kubernetes using LitmusChaos. Use ChaosEngine and ChaosExperiment custom resources to declaratively define experiments.
topics: Pod delete and pod CPU hog experiments
Node drain and node taint experiments
chaos-engineer.challenge: Deploy LitmusChaos in a Kubernetes cluster, run pod delete and network latency experiments on a sample microservice, and observe recovery via Prometheus.

chaos-engineer.title: Observability for Chaos Engineering
chaos-engineer.description: Build the observability foundation required to measure chaos experiment impact and confirm steady state. Implement distributed tracing to see how failures propagate across services.
topics: Correlation of chaos events with traces
chaos-engineer.challenge: Instrument a sample microservice with OpenTelemetry, create a Grafana dashboard showing SLI metrics, then run a Gremlin experiment and annotate the dashboard.

chaos-engineer.title: Network and Dependency Failure Testing
chaos-engineer.description: Simulate realistic network degradation and dependency failures to test service resilience patterns like circuit breakers, retries, and timeouts. Use service mesh for controlled traffic manipulation.
chaos-engineer.challenge: Configure Toxiproxy to inject 2-second latency and 10% packet loss on a service dependency, then validate that the circuit breaker opens and recovers correctly.

chaos-engineer.title: Database and Stateful Service Chaos
chaos-engineer.description: Test how applications handle database outages, disk failures, and stateful service disruptions. Validate data integrity and consistency after chaos events.
chaos-engineer.challenge: Simulate a PostgreSQL primary failure during active writes, validate replica promotion, verify data integrity, and document the recovery time observed.

chaos-engineer.title: Chaos Automation and GameDay Facilitation
chaos-engineer.description: Automate routine chaos experiments to run on a schedule and facilitate cross-team GameDay events that build resilience culture. Document learnings and track reliability improvements.
chaos-engineer.challenge: Plan and facilitate a 3-hour GameDay for a sample application, automate 3 scheduled experiments in GitHub Actions, and write a post-mortem document.

chaos-engineer.title: Production Chaos and Chaos Maturity
chaos-engineer.description: Graduate to running controlled chaos experiments in production environments with proper safety controls and on-call integration. Build a chaos engineering program that improves reliability over time.
chaos-engineer.challenge: Design a production chaos experiment with automated rollback triggers, PagerDuty integration, blast radius controls, and executive communication template.

accessibility-qa.title: Accessibility Fundamentals and WCAG
accessibility-qa.description: Build a foundation in web accessibility standards by studying WCAG 2.1 and understanding the four POUR principles. Learn about assistive technologies and who benefits from accessible design.
topics: WCAG 2.1 POUR principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust
WCAG conformance levels: A, AA, AAA
Accessibility vs usability vs inclusive design
accessibility-qa.challenge: Audit a public website against WCAG 2.1 AA manually, categorizing each finding by POUR principle and success criterion.

accessibility-qa.title: Screen Reader Testing with NVDA
accessibility-qa.description: Learn to navigate and test web applications using NVDA, the most widely used free screen reader. Understand how screen readers interpret HTML and what makes content accessible to blind users.
topics: Browse mode vs forms mode in NVDA
Navigating by headings, landmarks, and links
Testing form field labels and error messages
ARIA roles and their screen reader behavior
accessibility-qa.challenge: Navigate a sample web application using only NVDA and a keyboard, completing a form submission, and document all barriers encountered.

accessibility-qa.title: Keyboard Navigation Testing
accessibility-qa.description: Verify that all functionality is accessible using only a keyboard, which is required for both screen reader users and motor-impaired users. Test focus management, tab order, and keyboard traps.
topics: Tab, Shift-Tab, Enter, Space, Arrow key navigation
Custom widget keyboard patterns from ARIA Authoring Practices
accessibility-qa.challenge: Test an entire e-commerce checkout flow using only keyboard navigation, document every focus order violation and keyboard trap found.

accessibility-qa.title: Automated Accessibility Testing with axe
accessibility-qa.description: Use the axe accessibility engine to automate detection of common WCAG violations. Understand the scope and limitations of automated accessibility testing.
topics: Understanding axe incomplete and inapplicable results
accessibility-qa.challenge: Run axe-core on 5 pages of a web application, triage all violations by WCAG criterion, and calculate an accessibility score baseline.

accessibility-qa.title: Lighthouse Audits and Reporting
accessibility-qa.description: Use Google Lighthouse to perform automated accessibility audits and generate reports that communicate accessibility health to development teams. Track scores over time.
accessibility-qa.challenge: Set up Lighthouse CLI to audit 10 pages of an application, generate a trend report comparing scores across two code versions.

accessibility-qa.title: Color, Contrast, and Visual Accessibility
accessibility-qa.description: Test visual accessibility including color contrast ratios, use of color alone to convey information, and text sizing. Validate designs before development using design tool integrations.
topics: Zoom and text resize testing to 200 percent
accessibility-qa.challenge: Audit a sample application for all WCAG 1.4 contrast and visual presentation criteria, documenting fails with actual vs required contrast ratios.

accessibility-qa.title: Mobile Accessibility Testing
accessibility-qa.description: Test mobile applications for accessibility using iOS VoiceOver and Android TalkBack screen readers. Understand mobile-specific accessibility patterns and gestures.
topics: VoiceOver gestures and navigation on iOS
TalkBack gestures and navigation on Android
Switch access testing for motor impairments
accessibility-qa.challenge: Test a sample iOS app using VoiceOver and an Android app using TalkBack, completing the same user journey on both and documenting all accessibility failures.

accessibility-qa.title: ARIA and Semantic HTML Validation
accessibility-qa.description: Deeply understand ARIA roles, properties, and states and how they affect the accessibility tree. Test custom widgets for correct ARIA implementation and semantic HTML usage.
topics: ARIA roles: landmark, widget, document structure
Custom widget keyboard and ARIA patterns
accessibility-qa.challenge: Test a custom dropdown, date picker, and modal dialog for correct ARIA implementation using NVDA, documenting every ARIA violation found.

accessibility-qa.title: Accessibility Test Automation Integration
accessibility-qa.description: Integrate accessibility testing into your E2E automation suite so every page is scanned during functional test runs. Build accessibility regression prevention into the CI pipeline.
accessibility-qa.challenge: Add axe-playwright assertions to an existing Playwright suite covering all pages, configure exclusions for known issues, and generate an Allure report with violation details.

accessibility-qa.title: Accessibility Program and CI/CD Integration
accessibility-qa.description: Build an accessibility program that prevents regressions at every stage of the SDLC using CI/CD gates, developer tooling, and stakeholder reporting. Champion accessibility culture in your organization.
topics: Training developers and designers on accessibility
accessibility-qa.challenge: Configure Lighthouse CI in a GitHub Actions pipeline to block PRs that drop accessibility scores below 90, and write a VPAT for a sample application.

qa-lead.title: QA Leadership Foundations
qa-lead.description: Transition from individual contributor to QA leader by understanding the responsibilities, mindset shifts, and key competencies of a QA Lead. Learn to balance technical and people-management work.
topics: QA Lead vs Senior QA engineer role differences
Quality advocacy at the organizational level
Building credibility with development and product teams
Goal setting and OKRs for QA

resources.label: Engineering Management for the Rest of Us

qa-lead.challenge: Write a 30-60-90 day plan for a new QA Lead role, identifying key stakeholders, quick wins, and longer-term quality improvement initiatives.

qa-lead.title: Test Strategy and Planning
qa-lead.description: Craft a comprehensive test strategy that aligns QA activities with business goals and technical architecture. Translate strategy into actionable test plans for individual releases.
topics: Test strategy vs test plan differences
qa-lead.challenge: Write a complete test strategy document for a sample SaaS product covering scope, approach, environments, data, tools, risks, and resource requirements.

qa-lead.title: Building and Mentoring QA Teams
qa-lead.description: Learn to recruit, onboard, and grow QA engineers by providing structured feedback, coaching, and career development support. Build psychological safety within your team.
topics: QA engineer hiring: resume review, interview design
Onboarding plan for new QA engineers
Building a learning culture in QA
qa-lead.challenge: Design a QA engineer interview process with a take-home exercise, technical interview rubric, and behavioral question bank aligned to your team values.

qa-lead.title: QA Metrics and Reporting
qa-lead.description: Define and track meaningful QA metrics that demonstrate quality health and team effectiveness to stakeholders. Build executive-friendly dashboards that tell a story.
topics: Defect density and escape rate metrics
Weekly and release QA status reports
qa-lead.challenge: Design a QA health dashboard using Jira and Google Sheets that tracks 8 metrics across 4 categories and generates a weekly automated summary.

qa-lead.title: Agile QA Processes and Ceremony Ownership
qa-lead.description: Optimize QA activities within Agile sprints by embedding quality earlier in the process. Facilitate QA-related ceremonies and ensure testing never becomes a sprint bottleneck.
topics: Definition of Ready and Done with QA criteria
qa-lead.challenge: Design and facilitate a Three Amigos session for 3 user stories, capturing acceptance criteria, edge cases, and test scenarios as a team output.

qa-lead.title: Test Automation Strategy and Governance
qa-lead.description: Define an automation strategy that balances ROI against maintenance cost. Govern automation quality so the framework remains reliable and grows sustainably.
topics: Test automation pyramid and trophy strategies
Build vs buy automation tool decisions
Flaky test management and quarantine policy
qa-lead.challenge: Audit an existing automation suite, calculate its ROI, identify the top 10 flaky tests, and propose a governance policy document for your team.

qa-lead.title: Cross-Team Collaboration and Stakeholder Management
qa-lead.description: Develop skills to influence without authority, manage stakeholder expectations, and build quality advocacy across product, engineering, and leadership teams.
topics: Negotiating release dates and quality tradeoffs
Building trust with development team leads
Escalation paths for critical quality issues

resources.label: Influence Without Authority - Cohen and Bradford

qa-lead.challenge: Prepare and deliver a 10-minute QA status presentation for a mock executive audience that communicates release risk, key metrics, and a clear recommendation.

qa-lead.title: Incident Management and Root Cause Analysis
qa-lead.description: Lead post-incident quality reviews to extract learning and prevent recurrence. Build a blameless post-mortem culture that improves systems and processes.
topics: Root cause analysis techniques: 5 Whys, fishbone
Detection gap analysis: why tests missed this
qa-lead.challenge: Facilitate a blameless post-mortem for a simulated production incident, identify the detection gap in your QA process, and document 3 corrective actions.

qa-lead.title: Quality Culture and Continuous Improvement
qa-lead.description: Build a sustainable quality culture where quality is everyone responsibility, not just QA. Use continuous improvement frameworks to evolve your team processes over time.
topics: Quality culture vs quality control mindset
qa-lead.challenge: Design a quality champions program for a 20-person engineering team, including selection criteria, training plan, metrics, and quarterly review cadence.

qa-lead.title: QA Program Ownership and Strategic Leadership
qa-lead.description: Own the QA program at an organizational level by setting multi-quarter strategy, managing budgets, building the team roadmap, and representing quality at the leadership table.
topics: Building a testing center of excellence
Reporting quality health to C-level executives
qa-lead.challenge: Build a full QA program proposal for a growing startup including team structure, tool stack, automation strategy, metrics framework, and 12-month roadmap.
